architecture.summary: SyLeR empowers LLMs to perform explicit syllogistic legal reasoning through a three-stage pipeline: (1) tree-structured hierarchical retrieval mechanism that combines relevant legal statutes and precedent cases into comprehensive major premises, (2) supervised fine-tuning warm-up to establish foundational understanding of syllogistic reasoning, and (3) reinforcement learning with structure-aware reward mechanism to refine generation of diverse logically sound reasoning paths. The system bridges external legal documents (text) into the model via retrieval-augmented context, then steers the model's generation toward explicit syllogistic form through reward-shaped RL.

biblio.title: SyLeR: A Framework for Explicit Syllogistic Legal Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: Existing large language models can generate responses to legal questions but fail to perform explicit syllogistic reasoning, often producing implicit and unstructured answers that lack explainability and trustworthiness
SyLeR integrates a tree-structured hierarchical retrieval mechanism to effectively combine relevant legal statutes and precedent cases, forming comprehensive major premises
Supervised fine-tuning warm-up establishes a foundational understanding of syllogistic reasoning
Reinforcement learning with a structure-aware reward mechanism refines the model's ability to generate diverse logically sound and well-structured reasoning paths
SyLeR significantly improves response accuracy across in-domain and cross-domain user groups (legal laypersons and practitioners)
Results show SyLeR consistently delivers explicit, explainable, and trustworthy legal reasoning

Experiments span multiple dimensions: in-domain and cross-domain user groups (legal laypersons and practitioners), multiple languages (Chinese and French), and different LLM backbones (legal-specific and open-domain LLMs)
